---
title: "Connor Brown"
pagetitle: "Connor Brown"
description-meta: "Aquatic Ecologist | PhD Candidate | He/Him"
open-graph:
  description: "Aquatic Ecologist | PhD Candidate | He/Him"
twitter-card:
  description: "Aquatic Ecologist | PhD Candidate | He/Him"
page-layout: full
body-classes: home
css: assets/css/styles.css
---

<div class="profile-tagline">Aquatic Ecologist | PhD Candidate | <span class="pride-flag" role="img" aria-label="Progress Pride flag"></span> | He/Him</div>

::::: {.grid .profile-grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .profile-media}
<img id="rotating-img"
     src="assets/images/shn_deco.webp"
     alt="Connor with a post puller removing t-post from Shane Creek"
     class="rounded profile-photo" />


<nav class="social-icons" aria-label="Social links">
  <a href="https://github.com/ConnorB" target="_blank" rel="noopener" aria-label="GitHub">{{< fa brands github >}}</a>
  <a href="https://fediscience.org/@connor" target="_blank" rel="me noopener" aria-label="Mastodon">{{< fa brands mastodon >}}</a>
  <a href="https://instagram.com/conbro_" target="_blank" rel="noopener" aria-label="Instagram">{{< fa brands instagram >}}</a>
  <a href="https://bsky.app/profile/connorbrown.bsky.social" target="_blank" rel="noopener" aria-label="Bluesky">{{< fa brands bluesky >}}</a>
  <a href="https://x.com/eco_conn" target="_blank" rel="noopener" aria-label="X / Twitter">{{< fa brands twitter >}}</a>
  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ppjBxFEAAAAJ&hl=en" target="_blank" rel="noopener" aria-label="Google Scholar">{{< fa brands google-scholar >}}</a>
  <a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-9680-8930" target="_blank" rel="me noopener" aria-label="ORCID">{{< fa brands orcid >}}</a>
</nav>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8 .profile-bio}
I am a PhD Candidate at the [Kansas Geological Survey](https://kgs.ku.edu/water-resources-and-geohydrology) and [Department of Geology](https://geo.ku.edu) at the University of Kansas in [Dr. Erin Seybold’s](http://www.erinseybold.com/) lab, where I study ecosystem ecology and biogeochemical cycling in non-perennial streams as part of the Aquatic Intermittency Effects on Microbiomes in Streams (AIMS) project.

I previously completed an M.S. in Biology at Sam Houston State University, where I worked with [Dr. Amber Ulseth](https://nrri.umn.edu/faculty-staff/amber-j-ulseth-phd) on linking land use and precipitation regime changes to stream metabolism in subtropical Texas coastal streams. Before that, I earned a B.S. in Natural Resource Management (Fisheries and Aquatic Biology) at Texas Tech University, working with [Dr. Allison Pease](https://cafnr.missouri.edu/person/allison-pease/) and [Dr. Kelbi Delaune](https://meadowsresearchinstitute.sulross.edu/people/kelbi-delaune-ph-d/) on Pecos River macroinvertebrate communities.

My research focuses on carbon cycling in non-perennial streams. I am broadly interested in stream ecosystem ecology, hydrology, biogeochemistry, and data science.
:::
:::::
![](assets/images/con-header.webp){fig-align="center" fig-alt="Connor standing in Kings Creek doing sensor maintenance" loading="lazy"}

<script>
  const images = [
    { src: "assets/images/shn_deco.webp", alt: "Connor with a post puller removing t-post from Shane Creek" },
    { src: "assets/images/con_shn.webp",  alt: "Connor standing in an algae-covered pool next to a CO2 sensor" },
    { src: "assets/images/gbj.webp",      alt: "Connor smiling with an adult stonefly on his hand in Idaho" }
  ];
  let index = 0;
  const imgElement = document.getElementById("rotating-img");

  setInterval(() => {
    index = (index + 1) % images.length;
    imgElement.src = images[index].src;
    imgElement.alt = images[index].alt;
  }, 10000); // change every 10 seconds
</script>
